target.title: fix(agents): deliver native /compact replies through source suppression
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90185 by preserving visible replies for explicit native slash commands and by keeping compaction hook messages independent from the built-in `notifyUser` compaction notices.

This PR now covers the two confirmed #90185 paths:

1. Native `/compact` fast-path replies could return before the full inline-action command marker was applied. Channels using `message_tool_only` source-reply delivery then treated the terminal command reply as ordinary source content and suppressed it, producing `queuedFinal=false, replies=0` even though compaction ran.
2. Configured compaction hook messages could suppress the built-in `notifyUser` compaction notice because the notice lived behind an `else if` branch.

## Root cause

Native slash commands can be handled before workspace bootstrap and before the normal inline command path. The full inline command path already marks terminal command replies with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression`; the native slash fast path did not. That meant an explicit `/compact` result could be generated but later dropped by source-reply delivery policy in message-tool-only channels.

The compaction notice path had a separate branch-ordering issue: hook messages and the built-in `notifyUser` notice were mutually exclusive even though they are independent user-visible lanes.

## Changes

- Mark native slash fast-path replies with the same source-suppression bypass metadata used by the full inline command path.
- Apply the marker to native slash status replies, direct command-handler replies such as `/compact`, directive replies, and inline replies returned from the fast path.
- Keep compaction hook messages and built-in `notifyUser` notices independent.
- Update the stale compaction notice comment so it matches the new behavior.
- Add a regression test proving native `/compact` command-handler replies are marked for message-tool-only delivery.
- Extend fast-path tests to assert native slash status/directive replies carry the delivery marker.
- Keep bundled Feishu `/compact` routing coverage from the earlier patch.

## Validation

Local validation on the patched branch:

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check
# passed
```

Additional local attempts:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=verbose
# local environment reached the 180s timeout during Rolldown/Vitest build startup before assertion output

pnpm check:test-types
# local environment was stopped by SIGKILL during tsgo core test type checking
```

GitHub-hosted CI is the authoritative full validation for the pushed PR branch. For the current head, the fork CI run completed successfully and the upstream PR status checks show 0 failed and 0 pending checks.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: #90185 visible reply loss for native `/compact`, plus the related compaction notice lane where hook messages and built-in `notifyUser` notices must both remain deliverable.

Real environment tested: Linux local OpenClaw checkout running the current PR head `79e4ebf7abc5086bfe3530fa556806ed702f6385` as an isolated local gateway, connected by a WebChat WebSocket client. The model backend was a local mock OpenAI Responses provider so the proof did not depend on external model credentials. Gateway token, device identity material, loopback URL, and local filesystem paths are redacted/omitted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the PR-head gateway in isolated local mode, connected a WebChat WebSocket client, approved the local WebChat device, sent `/compact` through `chat.send`, waited for the final `chat` event, then read WebChat history. The captured command was `node --import tsx .artifacts/proof-90185-live-webchat.ts` against the current PR checkout.

Evidence after fix: Redacted live WebChat terminal output from the current PR head:

```json
{
  "head": "79e4ebf7abc5086bfe3530fa556806ed702f6385",
  "mode": "isolated local WebChat websocket live run",
  "request": {
    "sessionKey": "main",
    "message": "/compact",
    "idempotencyKey": "proof-90185-1780593374300"
  },
  "connect": {
    "ok": true,
    "payloadType": "hello-ok",
    "auth": "<redacted>"
  },
  "send": {
    "ok": true,
    "payload": {
      "runId": "proof-90185-1780593374300",
      "status": "started"
    }
  },
  "finalEvent": {
    "event": "chat",
    "payload": {
      "runId": "proof-90185-1780593374300",
      "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
      "state": "final",
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "⚙️ Compaction skipped: no real conversation messages yet • Context ?/128k"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "historySummary": {
    "ok": true,
    "messageCount": 2,
    "compactMessageCount": 2
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The current head delivered a visible WebChat assistant reply for `/compact` after dispatch: `⚙️ Compaction skipped: no real conversation messages yet • Context ?/128k`. WebChat history contained both `user: /compact` and the assistant compaction reply, so the old `message_tool_only` silent-drop path is no longer reproduced.

What was not tested: A live Feishu tenant screenshot/video was not attached. WebChat was used as the live transport-visible proof path requested by ClawSweeper, with sensitive runtime details redacted.

Current-head note: This proof was captured after rebasing on the latest upstream main and applying the required architecture-guard follow-up; the current head is `79e4ebf7abc5086bfe3530fa556806ed702f6385`. The follow-up only documents the migration prompt alias required by the guard and does not alter the `/compact` delivery path.

### Before-fix reproduction / root-cause proof

A source-level reproduction compared the current base (`upstream/main`) with this PR head:

```json
{
  "baseRef": "abb09b93cbc4c420a62a7ef6a376116cccc3313f",
  "headRef": "79e4ebf7abc5086bfe3530fa556806ed702f6385",
  "reproduction": {
    "baseNativeSlashFastPathHasDeliveryMarker": false,
    "headNativeSlashFastPathHasDeliveryMarker": true,
    "dispatchRequiresMarkerForSuppressedDelivery": true
  }
}
```

Relevant base excerpt from `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`:

```text
return { handled: true, reply: commandResult.reply };
return { handled: true, reply: directiveResult.reply };
return { handled: true, reply: inlineActionResult.reply };
```

That is the broken path: the native slash fast-path produced a terminal `/compact` reply, but returned it without the explicit-command delivery marker. Dispatch already requires `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` to bypass `message_tool_only` source suppression, so an unmarked native slash reply can be generated and then dropped.

Relevant PR-head excerpt:

```text
function markNativeSlashCommandReplyForDelivery(...)
reply: markNativeSlashCommandReplyForDelivery(...)
return { handled: true, reply: markNativeSlashCommandReplyForDelivery(commandResult.reply) };
return { handled: true, reply: markNativeSlashCommandReplyForDelivery(directiveResult.reply) };
reply: markNativeSlashCommandReplyForDelivery(inlineActionResult.reply)
```

### Hook messages + built-in notifyUser notices

The second #90185 path is covered by the focused regression test added in this PR:

```text
src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
- delivers compaction hook messages alongside notifyUser notices (#90185)
```

Expected visible sequence under `compaction.notifyUser: true` and hook-provided messages:

```text
Hook before
🧹 Compacting context...
Hook after
🧹 Compaction complete
```

This verifies hook messages are additive and no longer suppress the built-in `notifyUser` compaction notices.

### Validation after fix

Current-head local focused validation after rebasing on upstream `main`:

```text
pnpm exec oxlint \
  extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts \
  src/infra/json-file.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/json-store.ts \
  src/security/audit-config-include-perms.test.ts \
  test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts \
  src/security/audit-config-include-perms.test.ts \
  test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts
# extension-feishu: 74 passed
# auto-reply: 199 passed
# unit-fast security include permissions: 1 passed
# tooling lint suppressions: 3 passed

git diff --check
# passed
```

Current-head CI was automatically re-queued after the latest push. This section will be refreshed again if GitHub reports any new failure for `79e4ebf7abc5086bfe3530fa556806ed702f6385`.

## Risk / safety

- Narrow scope: only explicit native slash command replies returned by the fast path get the same delivery marker already used by the normal inline command path.
- Does not broaden ambient message delivery; the marker is applied only inside the native slash command fast path.
- Maintains existing `sendPolicy: deny` behavior because final dispatch still enforces send policy.
- Keeps room-event safeguards: dispatch still requires an explicit command turn for marked replies in room events.

## Notes

The issue references separate CardKit streaming rendering problems via related issues. This PR fixes the `/compact` reply-loss path and the compaction notice suppression path tracked here; it does not modify unrelated streaming card rendering code.

target.title: fix(memory-core): keep durable index identity visible during safe reindex
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90361.

`memory_search` intermittently returned `disabled/unavailable` with `index metadata is missing` during parallel or closely overlapping calls, even though the durable builtin index was valid (CLI deep status, direct DB inspection, and fresh-manager checks all showed valid metadata). The cause is a race: `MemoryIndexManager.search()` can kick off an async sync, and `runSafeReindex()` briefly swaps the live `this.db` to a fresh temp db (resetting vector/FTS state before metadata is written). A concurrent `status()` / tool-wrapper read during that window refreshes index identity from the temp db, finds no metadata, and fails closed with `index metadata is missing` — while the real index is fine.

## What changed

**`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts`** — add a `reindexInProgress` flag, set for the duration of `runSafeReindex`'s atomic temp-db swap and always cleared in a `finally`.

**`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`** — in `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, while `reindexInProgress` is set, a transient `missing` no longer overwrites a previously `valid` identity; the last stable identity is returned instead. Real mismatches — and `missing` outside a rebuild — still propagate, so genuine provider/model/settings drift keeps failing closed.

**`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`** — deterministic regression test: it drives a real safe (atomic) reindex and reads `status()` from inside the reindex build, after the temp db has been swapped in, asserting the durable identity stays `valid`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** A `status()` read concurrent with a safe reindex's temp-db swap keeps returning the durable `valid` identity instead of a transient `missing`, so `memory_search` no longer fails closed mid-rebuild. Real provider/model/settings mismatches and durable-missing states still fail closed.

**Real environment tested:** Local worktree of `origin/main` at base `118060157d`, Node v22.22.0. Drove the real `MemoryIndexManager` over a real on-disk SQLite index via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (the actual production reindex + status paths, no manager mocks). The concurrent `status()` is read from inside the real reindex build, while the real temp db is swapped in.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Concurrent status() during a real safe (atomic) reindex:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "concurrent status"
# Baseline measured by git-stashing the manager.ts guard and re-running the same test.
```

**Evidence after fix:** With the guard reverted (origin `manager.ts`), the same test fails — the concurrent read sees the temp db, reproducing the issue:
```
AssertionError: expected { status: 'missing', … } to deeply equal { status: 'valid' }
+   "reason": "index metadata is missing",
+   "status": "missing",
```
With the fix applied:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 34 skipped (35)
```

**Observed result after fix:** During a real safe reindex (temp db swapped in), `status().custom.indexIdentity` stays `{ status: "valid" }` instead of `{ status: "missing", reason: "index metadata is missing" }`. The transient `missing` reported by the issue is gone; durable-missing and real mismatch still surface.

**What was not tested:** No live OpenClaw v2026.6.1 end-to-end reproduction across multiple OS/providers; the change is in the deterministic manager identity-refresh path, exercised directly against the real manager and SQLite index. The fix is scoped to the manager identity layer (not a tool-wrapper-only mitigation), preserving real mismatch fail-closed as requested in the issue review.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `index.test.ts` + `tools.test.ts` + `manager-reindex-state.test.ts` → 59 passed; `manager.async-search.test.ts` → 3 passed
- `tsgo:extensions:test` → no new errors in changed files; `oxlint` → clean

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model limits from plugin.json via core
target.body: ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? - ANY Fireworks models from the catalog (which are only 2 Kimi variants by now) did not consistently apply their declared `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat`. The provider's `resolveDynamicModel` hook re-implemented catalog lookup in TypeScript and, when a catalog model was not already in user config, cloned the **default** template — flattening per-model limits (e.g. `kimi-k2.6` getting the 256000 default instead of its 262144). Why does this matter now? - The Fireworks plugin predates the `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest catalog. It carried a parallel TS catalog (`resolveFireworksCatalogModel` + a model `Map` + `buildFireworksModelFromCatalog`) that duplicated, and could drift from, the JSON. Core already has a canonical, plugin-agnostic path (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) that reads the same manifest with correct limits/compat — the plugin was preempting it. What is the intended outcome? - One canonical, JSON-driven resolution. `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` now **defers** manifest-declared ids (returns `undefined`) so core's bundled static catalog resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json`. The dynamic hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only thinking profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible `content` without it). What is intentionally out of scope? - No change to other providers, to core resolution code, nor to the gateway protocol. No new config/env surface. The manifest gains an explicit `reasoning:false` on the two Kimi rows (matching their off-only thinking profile); no other catalog content changes. What does success look like? - `kimi-k2.6` resolves with `contextWindow 262144` and `kimi-k2.5-turbo` with `256000` + its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords:["not"]` compat, with no code cloning the default template over a catalog model. Net non-test LOC down. What should reviewers focus on? - The resolution-order reasoning: explicit registry → plugin `resolveDynamicModel` → core static-catalog fallback (`resolveModelWithRegistry`, `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel`). Confirm deferring manifest ids correctly routes to core (the agent LLM path enables `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` in `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.ts`). - That `isFireworksManifestModelId` membership exactly matches the two catalog ids and Fireworks ids pass id-normalization unchanged. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes: no Related: no Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fireworks Kimi catalog models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting the default template's limits, and the Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. - **Real environment tested:** VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo --json` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture from the local setup): // kimi-k2p6 { "id": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "name": "Kimi K2.6", "provider": "fireworks", "contextWindow": 262144, "reasoning": false, "input": [ "text", "image" ], "compat": { "supportsDeveloperRole": false, "supportsUsageInStreaming": false, "supportsStrictMode": false } } // kimi-k2p5-turbo (default) { "id": "accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo", "name": "Kimi K2.5 Turbo (Fire Pass)", "provider": "fireworks", "contextWindow": 256000, "reasoning": false, "input": [ "text", "image" ], "compat": { "unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords": [ "not" ], "supportsDeveloperRole": false, "supportsUsageInStreaming": false, "supportsStrictMode": false } } - Observed result after fix: Each model surfaces its own distinct context window (262144 vs 256000) and kimi-k2.5-turbo keeps its compat — i.e. the default template is no longer flattening catalog limits. - What was not tested: Nothing — both the catalog `inspect` and the live `model run` (thinking off and high) were exercised on a real Fireworks key. On main, a not-yet-configured kimi-k2.6 resolved via the dynamic hook's default-template clone, yielding the 256000 default context window rather than 262144. - end-to-end evidence with an actual Fireworks key: ```bash # Resolves through the real runtime path; expect a normal answer with NO # … / chain-of-thought leaking into the visible content. openclaw capability model run \ --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 \ --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json ``` ```bash # /think no-regression: thinking level is clamped to the off-only profile; # the run still succeeds and still does not leak reasoning into content. openclaw capability model run \ --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 \ --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking high --json ``` - Evidence after fix (terminal capture from the local setup): // both cases { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "attempts": [], "outputs": [ { "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null } ] } Model correctly ignores "think" setting - Proof limitations or environment constraints: No limitaions nor constraints: capability model inspect reads the model catalog (which sources openclaw.plugin.json directly), so it confirms the JSON limits/compat are correct and surfaced; the end-to-end runtime resolution and the no-CoT-leak behavior are confirmed by the live capability model run above with a Fireworks key. ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts → 109 passed - pnpm test extensions/fireworks → 13 passed - pnpm check:test-types → exit 0 - node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/fireworks → exit 0 - pnpm format:check extensions/fireworks → clean - pnpm tsgo:extensions and pnpm tsgo:extensions:test → exit 0 - pnpm openclaw capability model inspect ... (see proof above) - .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --reviewers claude:claude-sonnet-4-6:medium ```bash autoreview target: local branch: fix/fireworks-plugin-config reviewers: claude model=claude-sonnet-4-6 thinking=medium tools: on web_search: on bundle: 13738 chars review still running: claude elapsed=60s pid=98131 autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported overall: patch is correct (0.93) The patch makes four coordinated changes to the Fireworks plugin: 1. **`reasoning: false` in manifest** — `openclaw.plugin.json` now explicitly declares `reasoning: false` for both `kimi-k2p6` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo`. This is purely additive and makes the static catalog authoritative for these models without any dynamic hook needed. 2. **Early-exit for manifest IDs in `resolveFireworksDynamicModel`** — The new guard at `index.ts:34` checks whether the requested model ID already appears in `FIREWORKS_MANIFEST_PROVIDER.models` and returns `undefined` if so, deferring to core's static-catalog resolution. The `modelId` used in the comparison is already `.trim()`'d at line 29, matching the string comparison semantics of the manifest IDs. This is correct. 3. **`isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`** — The function body is identical to the one it replaces in `index.ts`. This is a clean co-location refactor with no behavioral change. 4. **`structuredClone` replaces `cloneFireworksCatalogModel`** — The previous helper did a shallow clone of `model`, `input`, and `cost`. `structuredClone` performs a deep clone of the entire object tree, which is strictly more complete (handles the `compat` sub-object on `kimi-k2p5-turbo`, for example). No regressions here. Test updates correctly reflect the new contract: the deferred-manifest test verifies both manifest IDs return `undefined` from the dynamic hook, and the stream tests are reorganized to keep reasoning-field-stripping coverage intact (the stripping behavior for `kimi-k2p5` and `kimi-k2p6` with populated reasoning fields is explicitly tested in the loop-based test). No coverage gaps introduced. No bugs, security issues, or regressions found. ``` What regression coverage was added or updated? - extensions/fireworks/index.test.ts: new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test (asserts the dynamic hook returns undefined for both catalog ids); the "builds the Fireworks catalog" test continues to lock per-model reasoning/input/contextWindow/maxTokens from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained. - extensions/fireworks/stream.test.ts: kept the thinking:{type:"disabled"} assertions and onPayload chaining; removed the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip. - src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts: two resolver-level regression tests — (1) a deferred Fireworks manifest id (kimi-k2p6) resolves from core's bundled static catalog; (2) user openclaw.json config for the same id takes priority over the manifest (custom contextWindow wins, bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence: openclaw.json overrides openclaw.plugin.json field-by-field via core's mergeProviderModels / configuredModel-first fallback, matching every neighbor provider. What failed before this fix, if known? - Not a crash — a silently-wrong value: a not-yet-configured catalog model resolved with the default template's context window/limits instead of its own. If no test was added, why not? - Tests were added/updated (above). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes) - Catalog model resolution now applies correct per-model limits; the manifest marks the two Kimi rows reasoning:false (matches their off-only thinking profile). Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (No) - No new config/env keys, no migration. Resolution now defers to the existing core static-catalog path. Onboarded configs already carry these models (written from the same manifest) and continue to resolve via the registry. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (No) What is the highest-risk area? - Provider model resolution ordering: deferring manifest ids relies on core's static-catalog fallback being reached (allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true on the agent LLM path; configured providers also fall through resolveConfiguredFallbackModel). How is that risk mitigated? - Unit tests lock both the deferral and the precedence: extensions/fireworks/index.test.ts (plugin defers manifest ids) and src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts (core resolves a deferred manifest id via the bundled static catalog; user openclaw.json config overrides the manifest for the same id). core's resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel is independently tested with a bundled-manifest fixture; the live capability model run steps confirm end-to-end resolution and the no-CoT-leak stream behavior. ## Current review state What is the next action? - Maintainer review What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? - CI - Maintainer review Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - Addressed the rank-up review (preserve exact Fireworks manifest ID resolution for non-static-fallback callers; add resolveModelWithRegistry regression coverage). Verified user openclaw.json takes priority over the manifest in every path — explicit registry is consulted first (already user-priority-merged), and the fallback uses configuredModel ?? staticCatalog so a configured model short-circuits the manifest. Exact manifest ids resolve via core's bundled static catalog. No runtime code change required; added the two regression tests above as the lock. - Addressed the P1 "preserve manifest IDs outside static fallback paths" (extensions/fireworks/index.ts:34-36). The deferral is intentional and correct: these are discovery:"static" models (extensions/fireworks/openclaw.plugin.json:71), so they must resolve from the static catalog/registry, not be synthesized by the runtime dynamic hook — the old hook produced non-canonical metadata, which this PR fixes (see the added reasoning:false manifest rows). - Verified every non-opt-in runtime resolver path keeps these ids resolvable: - Agent run / btw / compact / gateway startup materialize the registry first (run.ts:717,734; btw.ts:257; compact.ts:589; gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:665); resolveExplicitModelWithRegistry finds the row via modelRegistry.find before the dynamic hook runs (embedded-agent-runner/model.ts:843). - /tools and gateway tools.effective consult resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel first (tools-effective-inventory.ts:205), which reads plugin manifests directly from disk with no sidecar dependency and returns discovery:"static" rows (embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.ts:188); sync resolveModel is only reached when that returns nothing. - openclaw models --provider fireworks lists these via runProviderStaticCatalog (commands/models/list.provider-catalog.ts:277), independent of the registry and resolveModelWithRegistry. - Verified user openclaw.json takes priority over the manifest in every path: the explicit registry is consulted first (already user-priority-merged via applyConfiguredProviderOverrides), and the static fallback short-circuits on a configured model (configuredModel ?? staticCatalog). Locked by the model.test.ts regression test asserting a user config row wins and the static catalog is never consulted. - Known cosmetic edge (not a runtime regression): the unfiltered `openclaw models` supplement loop (commands/models/list.rows.ts:464) uses resolveModelWithRegistry with no static-catalog step and readOnly loadModelCatalog does not generate models.json (model-catalog.ts:523), so on an agent dir where models.json was never generated the two static rows could be omitted from display. Pre-first-ensure only; the old code's dynamic hook would not have fired there either (no runtimeHooks passed, plugin is onStartup:false). - No runtime code change required; added two resolver-level regression tests in src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts as the lock

target.title: feat(cron): surface fallback progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop already-admitted model fallback chains from losing their embedded cron fallback candidate when Gateway drain begins; new queue enqueues still reject during drain.
- Add queue pressure fields to model fallback observations and surface cron primary/fallback progress into detached task run status.
- Sanitize fallback failure detail and persisted cron progress fields before task status/CLI surfaces can read them, including ANSI, OSC, C0, C1, and DEL control-character coverage.
- Strip 8-bit C1 OSC sequences in the shared ANSI helper and remove the full C1 control range in compact console sanitization.
- Replace the undefined docs example with source-backed OpenClaw artifacts (`cron task ledgers` and `command lane snapshots`).
- Rebase onto latest `upstream/main` and keep the inherited migration selection compatibility alias explicitly deprecated so architecture/deprecated-JSDoc passes.
- Thread cron task run ids through manual/isolated execution, add a reproducible runtime proof script, and document the production AI governance checklist.

## Verification

- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.task-progress.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.run-embedded.e2e.test.ts src/process/command-queue.test.ts` (3 shards, 52 tests)
- PASS: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- PASS: `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- PASS: `pnpm check:architecture`
- PASS: `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- PASS: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/utils/ansi.ts src/agents/console-sanitize.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.task-progress.test.ts src/commands/migrate/skill-selection-prompt.ts`
- PASS: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Already-active cron/background work can continue its configured model fallback chain after Gateway drain begins, including the embedded runner's session/global queue shape. New command enqueues still reject during drain. Cron-style task progress records primary/fallback model status plus queue active/queued/draining state, with fallback failure detail and terminal-control-bearing progress fields sanitized before persistence.
- Sanitizer repair after review: C1 OSC (`0x9d ... 0x9c`) is stripped by `stripAnsi`, and compact console sanitization removes the full C1 control range (`0x80`-`0x9f`) before cron progress is persisted. The cron progress regression now injects C1 OSC in provider/model/fallback detail and asserts no C1/control bytes or C1 OSC payload remain.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout at `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-87343-rebase`; PR head `b6249362d3eb0afecec24f011c44c2eb4b342a59`; rebased onto `upstream/main@fbac4a2ec77c84fb8c69f6498e6b1a6dec589c2d`; proof used isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/var/folders/_x/34p90_ts62zcl8g4bvfkyjsw0000gn/T/openclaw-pr-87343-proof-J4Vr6B`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
[proof] stateDir=/var/folders/_x/34p90_ts62zcl8g4bvfkyjsw0000gn/T/openclaw-pr-87343-proof-J4Vr6B
[proof] task created runtime=cron runId=pr-87343-cron-proof
[proof] initial progressSummary=model primary=openai/gpt-4.1-mini; queue active=0 queued=0 draining=no

[proof] attempt provider=openai model=gpt-4.1-mini allowGatewayDrainingContinuation=false
[proof] gateway drain marked while embedded queue work is active
[proof] fallback step={"fallbackStepType":"fallback_step","fallbackStepFromModel":"openai/gpt-4.1-mini","fallbackStepToModel":"anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","fallbackStepFromFailureReason":"rate_limit","fallbackStepFromFailureDetail":"primary rate limited","fallbackStepChainPosition":1,"fallbackStepFinalOutcome":"next_fallback","fallbackStepQueueActive":1,"fallbackStepQueueQueued":0,"fallbackStepQueueDraining":true}

[proof] PASS embedded queue-shaped fallback continued during drain; new enqueue still rejected

A new enqueue during drain was still rejected with `GatewayDrainingError`.

target.title: fix(update): hand off supervised auto-updates
target.body:
## Linked context

Closes #78492.

## Summary

- Route supervised startup auto-updates through the existing managed-service update handoff instead of spawning `openclaw update` inside the live gateway service process.
- Move the handoff implementation to `src/infra/` so both startup auto-update and `update.run` can share the same lifecycle code, while keeping the gateway import path as a re-export.
- Pass the configured update channel, timeout, and `OPENCLAW_AUTO_UPDATE=1` through the handoff command, keep the existing CLI package-update guard intact, and avoid creating an undeliverable restart continuation for unattended auto-update.

## AI assistance disclosure

I used AI assistance to inspect the issue, compare the prior closed PR, implement the patch, and run validation. I reviewed and understand the affected startup updater, managed-service handoff, restart scheduling, and CLI package-update guard paths.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: supervised gateway startup auto-update no longer runs the package update as a direct child of the live gateway service process with inherited service markers.

Real environment tested: local Linux source checkout with isolated temp state. The proof uses the real managed-service handoff helper and a fake updater command, not a live macOS launchd package update.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx .artifacts/issue-78492/proof-handoff.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```
✓ managed-service handoff created with correct channel (stable), timeout (120000), and AUTO_UPDATE=1
✓ handoff command does not inherit gateway service markers — safe to spawn inside live gateway
✓ handoff does not create an undeliverable restart continuation — no 'gateway service restarted by auto-update' wall message after unattended install
```

Observed result after fix: managed-service update handoff is created correctly with the expected channel, timeout, and environment marker, without service marker inheritance or restart continuation for unattended sessions.

What was not tested: uses a fake updater command, not a real macOS `launchctl` / `openclaw update` run. The handoff target, managed-service CLI argument parsing, and real update command behavior are outside this proof's scope.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ```
✓ without this patch, the gateway process printed an undeliverable 'gateway service restarted by auto-update' wall message and the supervisor saw a no-op restart that could stall further commands
```

## Tests and validation

```bash
pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test:fast  # all passing
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — unattended auto-update no longer produces an undeliverable restart continuation message, and the gateway process no longer runs the update command as a direct child with inherited service markers.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

What is the highest-risk area? The managed-service handoff path runs inside the live gateway service. If the handoff command fails or blocks, the gateway could stall or enter an inconsistent startup state.

How is that risk mitigated? The handoff uses the existing managed-service `spawnManagedService` helper with a timeout, same as `update.run`. The timeout prevents indefinite blocking, and the CLI package-update guard still applies on the handoff target.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper review — proof sufficient, rating 🐚 platinum hermit.

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress tagged `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` finals only after a final answer has actually delivered
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, tagged post-final warning suppression, and first-warning preservation

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal after a user-facing final has delivered.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Reviewed latest Codex P2 feedback on `finalAnswerDeliveryStarted` vs `finalAnswerDelivered`
- Gated tagged warning-final suppression on `finalAnswerDelivered`, while leaving tool-kind post-final suppression keyed to started or delivered final state
- Added regression coverage so a tagged non-terminal warning final remains visible when no final reply was delivered yet
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `d09e0740e5` and squashed into single commit `8a3514b61f`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, exec approval prompts, and tagged warning finals when no user-facing final reply has been delivered yet.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to then-current head `1a59a77093f9`. On 2026-06-04, this branch was rebased again onto `origin/main` `d09e0740e5` and force-pushed as current head `8a3514b61f` with the same one-commit PR surface plus the Codex P2 repair.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted` for head `1a59a77093f9`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `8a3514b61f`, matching the PR head after the latest rebase and P2 repair.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime came back online after the 2026-06-03 restart. The code P1 remains resolved; the Codex P2 has been addressed in current head `8a3514b61f`; the remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live Telegram behavior proof acceptance.
- What was not tested: I did not run a build, test, restart, Mantis desktop proof, or new live Telegram behavior probe after the 2026-06-04 rebase and Codex P2 repair to `8a3514b61f`, per operator instruction to ask before build/test/restart. The existing live proof does not include a Telegram transcript, recording, redacted runtime log, or artifact showing post-final non-terminal warning suppression and terminal warning preservation on the current head.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation, not a Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. A maintainer-triggered Mantis Telegram Desktop proof, equivalent redacted live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Post-squash maintainer/operator confirmation:
- Built and restarted live OpenClaw Telegram runtime on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC.

Post-2026-06-04 Codex P2 repair:
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `d09e0740e5` and pushed current head `8a3514b61f`.
- The commit hook formatted the two touched files during `git commit --amend`.
- No build, test, or restart was run after this repair because operator approval is required first.

## Current review state
- Code P1 is folded into current squashed head `8a3514b61f`
- Codex P2 is addressed in current head `8a3514b61f`
- Latest ClawSweeper review still requires stronger real Telegram behavior proof
- Mantis proof request was attempted, but skipped due contributor permission
- Waiting on maintainer-triggered Mantis, equivalent live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override before merge

target.title: fix(exec): reuse duplicate background sessions
target.body:
## Linked context

Closes #62432.

## Summary

- Repeated identical scoped background-capable `exec` retries now reuse the existing running process session instead of launching another child process.
- Reuse covers explicit `background: true`, explicit `yieldMs`, and the default auto-yield path where the model omits both fields.
- The match is conservative: command, resolved cwd, scoped process identity, reuse mode, and resolved execution options must match.
- Plain foreground reruns, completed reruns, env/PTY/timeout/cwd/scope/session drift, and normal `process` polling behavior remain separate.

## AI assistance

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed the change and understand the affected `exec` / `process` runtime path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Identical scoped `exec` calls with omitted `background` and `yieldMs` reuse the running default auto-yield session instead of spawning duplicate processes.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix-62432`, using the real `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` implementations with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. No live provider, channel, or credential state was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
mkdir -p .artifacts/pr-proof
proof_log=".artifacts/pr-proof/62432-20260601T142833Z-default-auto-yield.log"
OPENCLAW_EXEC_SHELL_SNAPSHOT=0 \
OPENCLAW_HOME="$(mktemp -d)" \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$(mktemp -d)" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$(mktemp -u)" \
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF' | tee "$proof_log"
import process from "node:process";
import { createExecTool, createProcessTool } from "./src/agents/bash-tools.js";
import { resetProcessRegistryForTests } from "./src/agents/bash-process-registry.js";

const scopeKey = "issue-62432-default-auto-yield-proof";
const command = "sleep 30";
resetProcessRegistryForTests();

const execTool = createExecTool({
  allowBackground: true,
  backgroundMs: 10,
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  notifyOnExit: false,
  scopeKey,
});
const processTool = createProcessTool({ scopeKey, cleanupMs: 60_000 });

const first = await execTool.execute("proof-default-yield-1", { command });
const second = await execTool.execute("proof-default-yield-2", { command });
const listed = await processTool.execute("proof-list", { action: "list" });
const runningSessions = (listed.details?.sessions ?? []).filter(
  (session) => session.status === "running",
);

console.log(`Evidence after fix: firstSessionId=${first.details?.sessionId}; secondSessionId=${second.details?.sessionId}; runningSessions=${runningSessions.length}`);
console.log(`Observed result after fix: reused=${first.details?.sessionId === second.details?.sessionId}; firstStatus=${first.details?.status}; secondStatus=${second.details?.status}`);

await processTool.execute("proof-remove", {
  action: "remove",
  sessionId: first.details?.sessionId,
});
resetProcessRegistryForTests();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:

```txt
Behavior addressed: identical exec calls with omitted background/yieldMs reuse the running default auto-yield session
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw exec/process tools via node --import tsx, isolated OPENCLAW_HOME/STATE/CONFIG, scopeKey=issue-62432-default-auto-yield-proof
Exact steps or command run after this patch: createExecTool({backgroundMs:10, scopeKey}).execute({command:"sleep 30"}) twice, then process list
Evidence after fix: firstSessionId=young-mist; secondSessionId=young-mist; runningSessions=1
Observed result after fix: reused=true; firstStatus=running; secondStatus=running
What was not tested: provider UI rendering, remote gateway/node host, and explicit human approval prompts
First response: Command still running (session young-mist, pid 72708). Use process (list/poll/log/write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.
Second response: Command still running (session young-mist, pid 72708). Use process (list/poll/log/write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.
Cleanup: Removed session young-mist (termination requested).
proof_log=.artifacts/pr-proof/62432-20260601T142833Z-default-auto-yield.log
```

Observed result after fix:
Both default auto-yield `exec` calls returned `status: "running"` with the same session id, and `process list` reported one running session.

What was not tested:
No live Xiaomi/MiMo replay or provider-driven agent turn was run. The proof exercises the runtime/tool path directly; GitHub CI covers the broader platform matrix.

## Root Cause

`handleExec` called `runExecProcess` before returning the running-session guidance. `runExecProcess` generated and registered a fresh process session for each call. The registry had id/list lookups, but no scoped same-execution lookup for an already-backgrounded in-flight process.

The issue is easiest to trigger when a model ignores the process follow-up guidance and repeats the same long-running `exec` call. Before this patch, OpenClaw treated that retry as a new launch.

## Dependency contract

External dependencies: none.

Source and docs checked:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts`
- `docs/gateway/background-process.md`
- `docs/tools/exec.md`

Contract used here: backgrounded sessions are managed through the `process` tool, are scoped by process identity, and are only pollable/listed when backgrounded. This patch stays inside that registry/tool contract and does not change public tool schemas.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added registry coverage for exact command/cwd/execution-key/reuse-key scoped matches, missing execution identity, mismatched reuse mode, mismatched scope/session, different command, different cwd, and foreground sessions.
- Added behavior coverage for repeated identical scoped explicit-background `exec` calls.
- Added behavior coverage for repeated identical scoped default auto-yield `exec` calls with omitted `background` and `yieldMs`.
- Added coverage for overlapping explicit background retries while the first spawn is still in flight.
- Added negative coverage that env, PTY, and timeout drift start distinct sessions.
- Added negative coverage that a plain foreground rerun is not collapsed into an active explicit-background session.
- Added runtime cleanup coverage for pre-spawn setup failures after an explicit background session is registered.

## Security Impact

No new permissions, network calls, credential handling, or approval bypass. The duplicate-session check runs after the existing exec target/security/workdir/approval path. Execution identity is stored as an opaque SHA-256 hash, not raw environment data.

## Human Verification

- Reproduced the pre-fix runtime behavior with two direct `runExecProcess` calls: `before_fix_running_sessions=2`.
- Ran the after-fix runtime proof above and reviewed the exec/process path.
- Confirmed the branch was rebased onto the refreshed `upstream/main` after local `main` and fork `origin/main` were safely fast-forwarded.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Checked `.github/CODEOWNERS`; the touched `src/agents/bash-*` files do not match the restricted auth/sandbox/secops entries. Expected review is normal agent runtime maintainer review.

## Changelog

No contributor `CHANGELOG.md` edit.

## Review conversations

- Target issue search found no linked or exact open PR for `62432`.
- Related closed/stale PRs were checked for fix shape and maintainer intent.
- A codex-spark worker was asked to inspect/implement; its partial edits were reviewed and corrected before integration.
- ClawSweeper/local review found concrete issues during drafting: option-drift reuse, pre-spawn phantom sessions, foreground rerun collapse, and missing default auto-yield coverage. This patch addresses them with code and tests.
- Final `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` returned clean.
- Standalone `codex review --base origin/main` was run after the final rebase, but the command timed out after 30 minutes before returning a verdict.

## Risks

An explicit background/yield/default-yield retry that intentionally wants a second identical concurrent process in the same scoped process identity needs a distinct execution option or command text. Plain foreground reruns are preserved.

## Behavior tradeoffs

The guard uses exact execution identity and an explicit reuse-mode key instead of fuzzy command similarity. That keeps the fix narrow and avoids guessing intent from generated text.

## Scope boundaries

- No provider-specific Xiaomi/MiMo routing change.
- No loop-detection threshold or config change.
- No process persistence, schema, docs, or public config change.
- Completed-session reruns and plain foreground reruns remain allowed.
- Unscoped ad hoc exec calls are not coalesced.

## Validation

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts` -> 94 tests passed
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` -> 3 Vitest shards passed
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> attempted; timed out after 30 minutes without a verdict
- Real runtime proof above

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress tagged `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` finals only after a final answer has actually delivered
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, tagged post-final warning suppression, and first-warning preservation

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal after a user-facing final has delivered.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Reviewed latest Codex P2 feedback on `finalAnswerDeliveryStarted` vs `finalAnswerDelivered`
- Gated tagged warning-final suppression on `finalAnswerDelivered`, while leaving tool-kind post-final suppression keyed to started or delivered final state
- Added regression coverage so a tagged non-terminal warning final remains visible when no final reply was delivered yet
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `d09e0740e5` and squashed into single commit `8a3514b61f`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, exec approval prompts, and tagged warning finals when no user-facing final reply has been delivered yet.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to then-current head `1a59a77093f9`. On 2026-06-04, this branch was rebased again onto `origin/main` `d09e0740e5` and force-pushed as current head `8a3514b61f` with the same one-commit PR surface plus the Codex P2 repair.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted` for head `1a59a77093f9`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `8a3514b61f`, matching the PR head after the latest rebase and P2 repair.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime came back online after the 2026-06-03 restart. The code P1 remains resolved; the Codex P2 has been addressed in current head `8a3514b61f`; the remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live Telegram behavior proof acceptance.
- What was not tested: I did not run a build, test, restart, Mantis desktop proof, or new live Telegram behavior probe after the 2026-06-04 rebase and Codex P2 repair to `8a3514b61f`, per operator instruction to ask before build/test/restart. The existing live proof does not include a Telegram transcript, recording, redacted runtime log, or artifact showing post-final non-terminal warning suppression and terminal warning preservation on the current head.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation, not a Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. A maintainer-triggered Mantis Telegram Desktop proof, equivalent redacted live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`

Post-2026-06-04 Codex P2 repair:

- The commit hook formatted the two touched files during `git commit --amend`.

target.title: fix(slack): emit message_sent hook on outbound delivery (mirror Telegram)
target.body:
## What

Adds the missing `message_sent` plugin hook emission to `@openclaw/slack`
outbound deliveries. Mirrors the Telegram adapter pattern
(`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts` →
`emitTelegramMessageSentHooks`).

Fixes #89942.

## Why

`docs/plugins/hooks.md` documents `message_sent` as observation-only for all
successful outbound deliveries. Telegram fires it; Slack silently doesn't.
Plugins observing the hook get a complete blind spot for Slack while
Telegram works out of the box.

## Change

Slack has two outbound reply paths and both needed the hook. The fix mirrors
Telegram's `emitTelegramMessageSentHooks` helper and emits on each.

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/slack/src/message-sent-hook.ts` | **New file.** `emitSlackMessageSentHooks` + internal-hook helper, a structural mirror of Telegram's pattern. Self-gates on `hookRunner.hasHooks("message_sent")`, so it is a no-op when no plugin observes the hook. Threads the canonical `sessionKey` into the hook context for cross-hook correlation. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.ts` | Non-streaming + streaming-fallback path. Wrap each `sendMessageSlack` (blocks, text, media) in try/catch: emit `message_sent` with the delivered `messageId` on success, emit `success=false` + rethrow on failure. New params thread the session key and group context through. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts` | Native-streaming happy path. Emit `message_sent` after `stopSlackStream` finalizes the streamed final reply, and `success=false` on a non-fallback stop failure. The streamed final content is captured only on the two genuine text-stream delivery points, so native-progress finals (which deliver through `deliverReplies`) are never double-emitted. Not emitted on the `SlackStreamNotDeliveredError` fallback branch (that re-enters `deliverReplies`, which already emits). |
| `extensions/slack/src/streaming.ts` | `stopSlackStream` now returns the finalized message `ts` from `chat.stopStream` (top-level `ts`, falling back to `message.ts`) so the streaming emit can populate `MessageSentEvent.messageId`, matching parity with the non-streaming path and Telegram. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/send.runtime.ts` | Re-export `SlackSendResult` so `replies.ts` can type the captured send result. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/replies.test.ts` | New `message_sent` tests (success with delivered `messageId`, `success=false` on throw, no-emit when no listener, internal-hook on session key, group-context threading, and `sessionKey` correlation in the plugin context), using a real `initializeGlobalHookRunner` registry with a real plugin hook handler; only the underlying Slack transport is stubbed. |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` | New emit-count regression tests: exactly-one emit for native-progress finals (no double emit), exactly-one finalizer emit for a plain text-stream final (with `sessionKey`), and no-double-emit on the `stopSlackStream`-throws and `appendSlackStream`-throws fallback branches. |
| `extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` | Tests for the new `stopSlackStream` return value (`messageId` from top-level `ts`, fallback to `message.ts`, empty result when neither is present) plus the updated return-type assertions. |
| `extensions/slack/src/outbound-delivery.test.ts` | Removed. This covered an earlier adapter-level approach that was dropped in favor of emitting on the two actual reply paths above. |

## Review response (ClawSweeper / Codex)

The automated review flagged two defects on the first commit; both are addressed in the latest commit and pinned with regression tests.

- **[P1] No double-emit for native-progress finals.** `streamedFinalContent` was captured unconditionally before the delivery branch, so in native-progress mode the final answer (delivered through `deliverReplies`, which emits) also triggered a second emit from the stream finalizer. Fixed by capturing the streamed final content only on the two genuine text-stream delivery points. Regression test: `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` -> "emits message_sent only once for native progress final replies (no double emit)" (verified failing on the pre-fix code).
- **[P2] `sessionKey` in the canonical sent context.** `buildSlackSentHookContext` now passes `sessionKey: params.sessionKeyForInternalHooks` into `buildCanonicalSentMessageHookContext`, matching the shared outbound emitter in `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts`, so plugins observing both `message_sending` and `message_sent` see the same `sessionKey`. Regression test: `replies.test.ts` -> "threads the session key into the message_sent plugin context for correlation" (asserts `sessionKey` on both the event and the context).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** When `@openclaw/slack` delivers an agent reply to Slack, the `message_sent` plugin hook never fires, even though `docs/plugins/hooks.md` documents it as firing for all successful outbound deliveries and the Telegram adapter emits it. A plugin observing the full outbound lifecycle receives `message_received`, `before_dispatch`, `agent_end`, `reply_payload_sending`, and `message_sending`, but never `message_sent`, despite the message landing in Slack. Root-cause analysis and the unpatched reproduction are in issue #89942.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v25.4.0. Runtime under test is this PR's latest commit built from source via `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`, which bundles `@openclaw/slack` directly from `extensions/slack/src/` (loaded as the stock `slack` plugin at `2026.6.2`), so it runs the exact TypeScript in this PR rather than an inline edit of an installed package. The gateway ran against an isolated state dir (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-fork-state`, a copy of `openclaw.json` + agent auth) so the live install was never touched. Channel was a real Slack workspace, real bot in a real channel (`C0APL029CEN`), Socket Mode, real `xoxb`+`xapp` tokens. Plugin `mst-probe` (local) listened on `message_sent` (and the surrounding lifecycle hooks), resetting a per-turn counter on `agent_end` and logging the emit count, `messageId`, and `sessionKey` (from both the event and the context) on each receipt.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```
  # Build the PR branch from source (bundles extensions/slack/src as the stock slack plugin)
  node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime

  # Run the built gateway against an isolated state dir (live install untouched)
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-fork-state node openclaw.mjs gateway

  # Register the mst-probe plugin (resets a per-turn counter on agent_end,
  # logs message_sent emit count + messageId + sessionKey).
  # Trigger a real agent reply by @mentioning the bot in #C0APL029CEN.
  # Repeat across the three delivery paths:
  #   1) streaming { mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }, plain reply  -> dispatch.ts text-stream finalizer
  #   2) streaming { mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }, tool call    -> native-progress -> deliverReplies (P1 check)
  #   3) streaming { mode: "off" }                                          -> deliverReplies
  ```

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** Live `mst-probe` console output from the built-from-source gateway, one real @mention -> agent reply per case. The probe reset its per-turn counter on `agent_end`, so `#1-this-turn` proves exactly one emit per delivered final:

  | # | Streaming config | Code path exercised | `message_sent` | `messageId` (real Slack `ts`) | `sessionKey` (event / ctx) |
  | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
  | 1 | `{ mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }` | `dispatch.ts` text-stream finalizer | `#1-this-turn`, `success=true` | `1780589381.301509` | present / present |
  | 2 | `{ mode: "partial", nativeTransport: true }` + tool call | native-progress -> `deliverReplies` | `#1-this-turn` (no double), `success=true` | `1780589561.093559` | present / present |
  | 3 | `{ mode: "off" }` | `monitor/replies.ts` -> `deliverReplies` | `#1-this-turn`, `success=true` | `1780589645.990569` | present / present |

  Raw probe line for the native-progress case (#2, the P1 scenario):

  ```
  [mst-probe] agent_end fired sessionKey=agent:main:slack:channel:c0apl029cen:thread:1780589549.718169 (turn counter reset)
  [mst-probe] message_sent FIRED #1-this-turn channelId=slack to=channel:C0APL029CEN success=true messageId=1780589561.093559 contentLen=96 event.sessionKey=agent:main:slack:channel:c0apl029cen ctx.sessionKey=agent:main:slack:channel:c0apl029cen
  ```

  The `messageId` values are real `chat.postMessage` / `chat.stopStream` timestamps returned by Slack for the delivered replies, confirming the hook carries the actual delivered message id on all three paths (parity with Telegram). The native-progress case fired exactly once (no double emit), and `sessionKey` is present on both the event and the context (P2). On unpatched stock `2026.5.28` the same probe never received `message_sent` (see issue #89942 for the unpatched capture). Supplemental automated coverage (not a substitute for the live proof above): `pnpm test:extension slack` -> 1249 passed; `pnpm test:contracts:channels` -> 194/38/29/34 passed; `pnpm check` -> typecheck + oxlint (0/0) + policy guards all pass.

- **Observed result after fix:** On the native-streaming text-stream path (`dispatch.ts` finalizer after `stopSlackStream`), the native-progress path (final delivered through `deliverReplies`), and the non-streaming path (`deliverReplies`), the registered `message_sent` listener received exactly one event per delivered final with `to`, `content`, `success=true`, `messageId=<real Slack ts>`, and the canonical `sessionKey` on both the event and the context. The native-progress case did not double-emit. Send failures emit `success=false` with `error` and then rethrow (unit-tested). Suppressed `NO_REPLY` sends correctly skip emission. This mirrors how the Telegram adapter behaves today.

- **What was not tested:** Live failure injection (`success=false`) was not captured live to avoid leaving the Socket Mode connection in a bad state; it is covered authoritatively by the unit test "emits message_sent with success=false when delivery throws" plus the dispatch finalizer catch branch. The internal `message:sent` hook is wired conditionally on a caller-provided `sessionKeyForInternalHooks`; the two `deliverReplies` callers pass `route.sessionKey`, but the standalone CLI `message send` path (`slackChannelOutbound.sendText`) is intentionally out of scope here since it does not go through the agent reply paths this PR targets. Happy to follow up.

- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Verification used an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` copy of the live config so the production install was never modified; the launchd-managed live gateway was temporarily stopped to free the Slack Socket Mode connection for the built-from-source gateway, then restored afterward.

## Notes

- AI-assisted (Claude). Real-behavior reproduction and root-cause
  analysis were done with a custom `mst-probe` plugin on a clean
  local install.
- `CHANGELOG.md` not modified, per CONTRIBUTING.md.
- American English in all new code and comments.
- Allow edits by maintainers enabled on the fork branch.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511
