target.title: feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds two plugin observability hooks for model failure handling:

- `model_failover`: observes embedded-runner failover decisions, including failed source refs, selected target refs, stage, decision, reason metadata, recoverability, timeout/abort/status flags, and fallback availability.
- `model_failure_terminal`: observes terminal before-reply model failures after all configured attempts are exhausted.

This PR does not change fallback policy, provider retry behavior, context re-injection behavior, command/tool execution, network access, or default user-facing failure copy.

## Current implementation

- Emits `model_failover` from the embedded-runner failover decision path.
- Propagates the selected fallback candidate through both direct agent-command runs and the Gateway auto-reply path before running the embedded agent.
- Emits `model_failure_terminal` after resolving the actual delivered failure reply, so `finalMessage` matches user-visible failure copy.
- Registers both hook names and payload types in the Plugin SDK surface and refreshes the SDK API baseline hash.
- Bounds both failure-path void hooks with default 30s timeout budgets and fail-open behavior.

## Security / compatibility

- New public Plugin SDK hook contract: `model_failover`, `model_failure_terminal`.
- `model_failure_terminal` is conversation-access gated for non-bundled plugins because it can expose delivered failure copy.
- Attempt summaries expose provider/model/reason metadata, not raw provider error blobs.
- Maintainers still need to accept the public hook contract and terminal-failure data boundary before release.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A real embedded OpenClaw model run should emit `model_failover` when the primary model fails and the runtime selects the configured fallback target. A separate exhausted-model run should emit `model_failure_terminal` after all configured model attempts fail, with `finalMessage` matching the user-visible failure copy and `attempts` summarizing provider/model/reason metadata.
- **Environment**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS Darwin 25.3.0 arm64, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, PR head `6f90acd4df38`.
- **Setup**: The proof runner created a temporary workspace, temporary state dir, and a real workspace plugin with `openclaw.plugin.json`. OpenClaw loaded that plugin through the real plugin loader; the plugin registered `api.on("model_failover", ...)` and `api.on("model_failure_terminal", ...)` and wrote hook payloads to a redacted JSONL artifact. Model providers were deterministic local OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoints. Scenario 1 returned HTTP 429 from the primary endpoint and a successful SSE canary from fallback. Scenario 2 returned HTTP 429 from both primary and fallback to exhaust all configured model attempts. No external provider token was used.
- **Command**:

```bash
OPENCLAW_MODEL_FAILOVER_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx .artifacts/model-failover-real-proof.mjs
```

- **Observed result**:
  - `model_failover` fired during the real embedded run after `proof-primary/proof-primary-model` returned 429; the event reported selected target `proof-fallback/proof-fallback-model`, failed source `proof-primary/proof-primary-model`, `decision: fallback_model`, `failoverReason: rate_limit`, `status: 429`, and `sourceRecoverable: true`.
  - The fallback endpoint was hit and the run delivered `PROOF_FALLBACK_CANARY_OK`.
  - The terminal run hit both `proof-terminal-primary/proof-terminal-primary-model` and `proof-terminal-fallback/proof-terminal-fallback-model`, exhausted all attempts, and emitted `model_failure_terminal` with `kind: all_models_failed`.
  - `model_failure_terminal.finalMessage` was exactly the delivered terminal reply: `⚠️ All models are temporarily rate-limited. Please try again in a few minutes.`
  - Terminal `attempts` contained both failed provider/model summaries with `rate_limit` reasons.
- **Limitations**: Provider failures are deterministic local HTTP endpoints, not live third-party outages. Secret-bearing headers are redacted in proof output.

## Regression coverage for the latest review findings

New source tests cover the two ClawSweeper P1 findings directly:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: `passes the selected next fallback candidate into embedded auto-reply runs`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: `emits model_failure_terminal with the delivered terminal failure copy`

The unrelated Anthropic config-default change is no longer part of this PR diff.

## Validation

Commands run on head `6f90acd4df38`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:architecture
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
OPENCLAW_MODEL_FAILOVER_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx .artifacts/model-failover-real-proof.mjs
```

Observed results:

- Focused Vitest passed 3 shards: auto-reply 184 tests, agents 85 tests, plugins 14 tests.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed.
- `tsgo:test:src`, `pnpm check:architecture`, `git diff --check`, and focused `oxfmt --check` passed.
- Exact-head real behavior proof passed with selected fallback target and terminal delivered-copy assertions true.

## Linked issue

Closes #70976
Related #65824

target.title: feat(sessions): add adaptive reset mode with daily AND idle semantics
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Session reset currently supports only `daily` and `idle`, and when both are configured the behavior is OR-based, which can reset an active conversation as soon as the daily boundary passes.
- Why it matters: Some users want the daily rollover guard without interrupting active conversations, meaning reset should happen only after the boundary has passed and the session has gone idle.
- What changed: Added `mode: "adaptive"` as a narrow reset mode that requires both the daily boundary and the idle window before a session becomes stale, and updated the schema/help/session wiring to match.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add new reset fields, change the default reset mode, or alter existing `daily` and `idle` semantics.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #43738
- Related #44489
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `session.reset.mode` accepts a new value: `"adaptive"`.
- `adaptive` resets a session only when both conditions are met:
  - the daily reset boundary has passed
  - the configured idle window has expired
- Existing `daily` and `idle` behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[daily stale] OR [idle stale] -> reset

After:
mode=daily/idle -> existing behavior unchanged
mode=adaptive -> [daily stale] AND [idle stale] -> reset
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): session reset policy with `atHour` and `idleMinutes`

### Steps

1. Configure session reset mode as `adaptive` with `atHour` and `idleMinutes`.
2. Evaluate freshness when only the daily boundary is crossed.
3. Evaluate freshness when only the idle window expires.
4. Evaluate freshness when both are true.

### Expected

- Daily-only stale: session remains fresh.
- Idle-only stale: session remains fresh.
- Both stale: session resets.
- Existing `daily` and `idle` modes behave exactly as before.

### Actual

- Added adaptive-mode policy tests plus schema/session behavior coverage.
- Updated schema/help/generated config surfaces and session handling.
- Focused Vitest suite passed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `session.reset.mode: "adaptive"` should keep an existing session fresh when only the daily boundary or only the idle window is stale, and should roll the session only when both conditions are stale.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, running this branch's real `initSessionState` path against a temporary on-disk session store under `~/.openclaw/state/tmp`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a one-shot runtime script from the branch with `pnpm exec tsx` that seeded three real session rows, configured `session.reset.mode: "adaptive"`, and called `initSessionState` for daily-only, idle-only, and both-stale cases.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that real runtime path:

  ```text
  OpenClaw adaptive reset runtime proof
  tempStore=/Users/mantis/.openclaw/state/tmp/openclaw-adaptive-real-proof-hn8QYH/sessions.json
  policy=mode:adaptive atHour:8 idleMinutes:60
  daily-only stale stays fresh: isNewSession=false previous=adaptive-daily-only result=adaptive-daily-only
  idle-only stale stays fresh: isNewSession=false previous=adaptive-idle-only result=adaptive-idle-only
  daily+idle stale rolls session: isNewSession=true previous=adaptive-both-stale result=45327a3a-ef16-44a5-a950-96a8bc242f29
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The actual session runtime preserved the current session when only one adaptive condition was stale, and created a new session only when both daily and idle conditions were stale.
- **What was not tested:** Full interactive Telegram delivery flow; this proof targets the real OpenClaw session initialization/reset path that owns the adaptive rollover decision.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - adaptive policy stays fresh when only one side is stale
  - adaptive policy resets when both daily and idle conditions are stale
  - schema accepts `mode: "adaptive"`
  - session-related tests still pass with the new mode wired through
- Edge cases checked:
  - adaptive mode defaults an idle window when omitted in policy resolution
  - schema/help/generated config surfaces remain aligned
- What you did **not** verify:
  - remote GitHub CI beyond the local focused suite and `check:changed`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: AND/OR freshness logic could be implemented incorrectly and silently change reset behavior.
  - Mitigation: Added focused policy tests for both-one-neither stale combinations while preserving existing mode behavior.
- Risk: Config/schema layers could drift if `adaptive` is added in one validation surface but not another.
  - Mitigation: update type union, Zod schema, generated schema/help text, and session wiring together.
- Risk: stale-session reason/reporting paths could become misleading if adaptive mode is only partially wired.
  - Mitigation: included session-related tests and the current session/reset handling surfaces in the same change.

## Latest local validation

Current head: `b61b8af26c1e2903afec2810937d2ac3dc24c78e`

Branch history: rebuilt from current upstream into one feature commit and force-pushed after dropping stale rebase/sync churn.

Local validation on the squashed head:

- `pnpm config:docs:check` — passed
- `pnpm test src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session-hooks-context.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm deadcode:unused-files` — passed
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` — passed

GitHub checks on the current head while updating this proof:

- `gh pr checks 71400 --repo openclaw/openclaw --json name,state,workflow` — 124 success, 28 skipped, 1 neutral, 0 failing, 0 pending
- `gh pr view 71400 --repo openclaw/openclaw --json mergeable,headRefOid` — head matches `b61b8af26c1e2903afec2810937d2ac3dc24c78e`; mergeability currently reports `MERGEABLE`

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent turn end hook
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a clean `agent:turn:end` internal hook event for ACP runtime turn completion. This split intentionally keeps the event as a runtime lifecycle boundary only; it does not claim transcript/session-write durability.

## Changes
- Registers and validates `agent:turn:end` hook events with `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` context.
- Emits the event from the ACP manager after terminal runtime turn handling and awaited ACP event-delivery callbacks.
- Does not emit `agent:turn:end` for pre-runtime session initialization failures such as max-concurrency rejection.
- Documents the lifecycle-only contract and explicitly states that this event does not prove durable session writes.
- Adds focused ACP manager and internal hook tests for success, failure, ordering, and pre-runtime init-failure gating.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: proves the clean split branch emits `agent:turn:end` from an actual ACP runtime turn, while keeping save/write-durability concerns out of this PR and avoiding turn-end emission for pre-runtime init failures.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at `/Users/mantis/Projects/openclaw-pr-turn-end-clean`, branch `feat/agent-turn-end-hook-clean`, rebased onto current `origin/main`, head `52d28708c0`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the source-built Gateway from this branch with a temporary config/state directory, enabled the embedded `acpx` runtime plugin, loaded a temporary internal hook through `hooks.internal.load.extraDirs`, initialized a real ACPX/Codex persistent session using a temporary copied `CODEX_HOME`, ran `AcpSessionManager.runTurn()` with the prompt `Reply with exactly TURN-END-HOOK-PROOF-OK`, then attempted a second runtime session while `acp.maxConcurrentSessions` was `1` to force a pre-runtime `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` path.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```text
$ git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD
52d28708c0
$ OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_CODEX_MODEL=gpt-5.5 \
  OPENCLAW_TURN_END_SOURCE_CODEX_HOME=/Users/mantis/.codex \
  OPENCLAW_TURN_END_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD) \
  node --import tsx .artifacts/turn-end-proof-run.mjs
{
  "ok": true,
  "head": "52d28708c0",
  "initializedBackend": "acpx",
  "successSessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-proof-f0e595eb-8a63-42f0-80aa-b6e373699f0c",
  "blockedSessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-blocked-4cdae395-c82e-4e0f-b466-ef6a1e0f1486",
  "successHookContext": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:proof-codex:acp:turn-end-proof-f0e595eb-8a63-42f0-80aa-b6e373699f0c",
    "success": true,
    "durationMs": 4200
  },
  "blockedError": {
    "name": "AcpRuntimeError",
    "code": "ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED",
    "message": "ACP max concurrent sessions reached (1/1)."
  },
  "hookEventCount": 1,
  "blockedHookEventCount": 0,
  "proofLog": "/Users/mantis/.openclaw/state/tmp/openclaw-turn-end-proof-wuPoN6/turn-end-proof.jsonl"
}
```
- Additional focused validation:
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 6.99s
$ pnpm check:test-types
<exit 0>
$ pnpm exec oxlint src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
<exit 0>
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test-helpers.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
<exit 0>
```
- Observed result after fix: the temporary hook captured exactly one real `agent:turn:end` event from a live ACPX/Codex turn with `success: true`; the pre-runtime max-concurrency failure returned `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` and captured zero `agent:turn:end` events for that blocked session.
- What was not tested: production gateway deployment. This was intentionally run against a temporary local Gateway/config/state using source code from this branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses local Codex auth copied into a temporary Codex home and a one-off local proof harness that is not committed; hook handlers are internal and only observable when an operator installs/enables a hook.

## Testing
- Real source-built ACPX/Codex turn proof described above

commands.author: Bluetegu

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add readOnly account mode
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- WhatsApp accounts had no way to operate in a purely passive / monitoring mode — any triggered session may send replies to the chat, with no deterministic mechanism to suppress all outbound writes.

Why does this matter now?

- Enables non-intrusive monitoring, archival, and ambient-awareness use cases (e.g. connecting an account to observe a group without the risk of the agent responding). 

What is the intended outcome?

Setting `readOnly: true` on an account puts it into passive observation mode. OpenClaw receives
and processes inbound messages normally but never writes anything back to WhatsApp on that account:

- **Outbound messages are always suppressed** — no message, poll, or reaction will ever be sent,
  regardless of trigger (agent reply, agent initiative, or explicit owner request).
- **Typing indicators are suppressed** — neither the connect-time presence announcement nor
  per-turn composing presence is sent.
- **Owner slash commands are silently dropped** — commands like `/reset` are interactive and
  confirm via a reply; on a read-only account that reply can never be sent, so the command is
  dropped before processing.
- **Read receipts are independent** — governed by `sendReadReceipts` separately from `readOnly`.

Typical use: a personal number used to read and summarize conversations without ever replying on
your behalf; or a compliance / archive account that must not send under any circumstances.

Feature documentation was added to the WhatsApp channel docs.

Developed with the help of github co-pilot using Claude Sonnet 4.6

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No runtime toggle — changing `readOnly` requires a gateway restart (same lifecycle as all account-level config).
- No new agent tool to set `readOnly`. By design, `readOnly` is not in the `assertGatewayConfigMutationAllowed` allowlist, so agents cannot mutate it.
- No UI surface — config only.

What does success look like?

- Agent sessions run normally (`prompt.submitted` → `model.completed`), tools are called, model generates replies — but nothing reaches the WhatsApp socket when `readOnly: true`. Gateway log shows `Dropped X (read-only)` lines at `info`, no `Sent X` lines. Removing the flag restores normal behavior with `Sent X` confirmations.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Guard coverage across all outbound paths: `send-api.ts` (`sendMessage`, `sendPoll`, `sendReaction`), `monitor.ts` (connect presence, pre-dispatch gate), `deliver-reply.ts` (auto-reply path), `send.ts` (`sendMessageWhatsApp`, `sendReactionWhatsApp`, `sendPollWhatsApp`).
- The `dropReason` propagation chain added to `WhatsAppReplyDeliveryResult` in `deliver-reply.ts`, and whether the unified `normalizeWhatsAppSendResultDropped` helper in `send-result.ts` is the right abstraction boundary.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #78963 (partial — this PR covers the passive / silent-account part of the request).
Other requests in that issue not involving the LLM can be addressed in separate PRs.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Owner implementation.


## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** WhatsApp account silences all outbound sends when `readOnly: true`; gateway log confirms drops at `info` with `dropReason: "read-only"`; normal sends still confirmed when `readOnly: false`.
- **Real environment tested:**
  - macOS
  - Model: `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Toggled `readOnly` in `openclaw.json`, restarted gateway, triggered agent turns from WhatsApp group or direct channel.
- **Evidence after fix:** screenshot comparisons and gateway log excerpts — see Screen Capture and Gateway log sections below
- **Observed result after fix:** All live test areas passed.
- **What was not tested:** Poll sending (`sendPollWhatsApp`) — no live test; covered by code inspection and same guard/log pattern as reactions. The implicit auto-reply pipeline (`reply()` / `sendMedia()` closures in `inbound/monitor.ts`) was not live-tested — the model always uses the explicit `message` tool; covered by unit tests in `deliver-reply.test.ts` and `monitor-inbox.read-only.test-support.ts`.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** `readOnly: true` composing suppression has no app-level log; behavioral confirmation only (user observed no typing indicator on device.

Configuration used in the tests:
```json
  "channels": {
    "whatsapp": {
      "enabled": true,
      "accounts": {
        "test": {
          "readOnly": false,  // toggeled
          "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
          "groupAllowFrom": [
            "*"
          ],
          "groups": {
            "120363406415684625@g.us": {
              "requireMention": false,  // modified
              "systemPrompt": "Be nice!"
            }
          },
          "debounceMs": 0
        }
      }
    }
  }
```
**Manual test summary**

| #   | Area                            | Passes run                         | Outcome                                                                     |
| --- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Text reply                      | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes                                                               |
| 2   | Media reply                     | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes                                                               |
| 3   | Composing indicator             | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes (behavioral; no app-level log)                                |
| 4   | Reaction sending                | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes                                                               |
| 5   | Control command drop            | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes                                                               |
| 6   | DM access-control pairing reply | `readOnly=true`                    | ✅ Pairing reply suppressed — `sendTrackedMessage` catch-all guard exercised |

---

### Execution & Integration Logs

Captured 2026-06-03, account `whatsapp-account-test`, model `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`,
gateway restarted between `readOnly=false` and `readOnly=true` passes for every test.

---

#### Test 1 · Text reply

**Purpose:** Verify that the primary `sendMessageWhatsApp` send path in `send.ts` drops the outbound message and logs `Dropped message (read-only)` when `readOnly=true`, while model execution and read receipts are unaffected.
**Expected `readOnly=false`:** `Sent message <id>` log line with a real WA message ID; round-trip latency in the hundreds of ms (socket write to server).

**Expected `readOnly=true`:** `Dropped reaction (read-only) "👍"` log line; no reaction on device; no `Sent reaction` line.

**Purpose:** Verify that owner slash commands (e.g. `/reset`) are silently dropped before entering `onMessage()` / `process-message.ts` when `readOnly=true`. These commands confirm via a WhatsApp reply; since that reply can never be sent, the command must not run at all.
**Expected `readOnly=false`:** `inbound web message` log line; command processed; `Sent message` confirmation of the `✅ Session reset…` reply.
**Expected `readOnly=true`:** No `inbound web message`; no `session turn created`; no `Sending message` / `Sent message`. Gateway goes idle. Drop is silent (pre-processing gate, not a post-processing `Dropped` log).

#### Test 6 · DM access-control pairing reply — `readOnly=true`

**Purpose:** Verify the `sendTrackedMessage` catch-all guard in `monitor.ts` (the sole guard on the DM access-control path, which bypasses `send-api.ts` entirely). When an unauthorized DM triggers an "Access not configured" pairing reply, the reply must be suppressed when `readOnly=true`.
**Expected `readOnly=true`:** `whatsapp pairing request` logged (access control evaluated the sender); no `Sending message` / `Sent message` for that sender; pairing reply not received on device.

An unauthorized DM arrived while `readOnly=true` was active. `sendTrackedMessage` catch-all
guard at `monitor.ts:432` is the sole guard on this path (it bypasses `send-api.ts` entirely).

```
17:51:48+03:00 verbose [whatsapp/access-control]  whatsapp pairing request sender=… name=…
17:51:48+03:00 debug  [whatsapp/inbound]           Skipping recent outbound WhatsApp echo 3EB06E56A3AF4F292C9F1E for …@lid
```
→ `whatsapp pairing request` logged — access control ran and evaluated the sender.
NO `Sending message` / `Sent message` for this sender anywhere in the log.
"Access not configured" pairing reply was NOT received on device — `sendTrackedMessage` guard suppressed the Baileys `sock.sendMessage()` call.
(The echo log entry carries an inbound-echo ID from an earlier session, not a new outbound send.)

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp                                                    # 860 tests, 82 files — all passing
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts
pnpm check
pnpm check:test-types                      
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `whatsapp-web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts` — new E2E test covering `readOnly` drop logging for the auto-reply path.
- `deliver-reply.test.ts` — new unit tests for `dropReason` propagation in `WhatsAppReplyDeliveryResult`.
- `monitor-inbox.read-only.test-support.ts` — shared test-support fixture for `readOnly` inbound scenarios.
- `send.test.ts` — new boundary-level `it.each` test asserting that a policy drop from `sendMessageWhatsApp` and `sendPollWhatsApp` returns `messageId: ""`, not the internal `"dropped"` sentinel.

What failed before this fix, if known?

N/A — new feature, not a bugfix PR.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — tests were added.


## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — new behavior when `readOnly: true` is set: the account goes silent. No change for existing accounts (flag is optional, absence is equivalent to `false` / current behavior).

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — new optional `readOnly` boolean field on WhatsApp account config in `openclaw.json`. Defaults to `false` (existing behavior preserved). No migration needed; no existing config files are affected.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

## Summary
- Adds opt-in Slack message actions for search, channel discovery, permalinks, scheduled and ephemeral delivery, files, bookmarks, reminders, and canvases.
- Adds Slack send controls for reply broadcast and unfurl preferences.
- Gates Slack search discovery on user-token availability and documents the new config surface.

## Verification
- pnpm test extensions/slack/src/actions.canvas.test.ts extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/message-tools.test.ts

target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
